markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define what local draft state may exist before promotion and how it is recovered after crash/restart. This MT belongs to CRDTAndConcurrencyCore. Make rich document, graph mutation, and agent proposal state conflict-aware and recoverable.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
CRDT requirements apply to human plus agent concurrent draft state, rich document block state, graph mutation proposals, and EventLedger promotion, even when realtime multi-user UI is deferred.
AI/agent actions must carry stable actor/site IDs, leases, idempotency keys, and recovery receipts.
scope.expected_behavior: OfflineDraftStateBoundary leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: OfflineDraftStateBoundary is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 f53a9cae; CRDTAndConcurrencyCore: 41 tests/6 binaries green on real PostgreSQL. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: OFFLINE REPLAY CANNOT REACH AUTHORITY (the #6 offline variant): replay_offline_envelopes (offline_boundary.rs:163-225) routes EVERY buffered envelope through the same push_yjs_update draft-log path. It physically cannot write promoted_facts, promotion events, or proposal review_state -- replay is draft-only. The boundary contract's replay_must_not_mutate list (offline_boundary.rs:74-79) names exactly those authority surfaces, and the code honors it by construction (no authority writers in the replay loop).

NO DRAFT LOSS / IDEMPOTENT: every envelope gets a typed verdict (Stored | AlreadyStored | Denied) with counts -- no silent drops. Re-submitted already-stored updates dedup (AlreadyStored); stale ones get a typed Denied carrying the reason so the client rebases and resubmits exactly the failed tail.

SELF-REFERENTIAL CONTRACT VALIDATOR (note): validate_offline_draft_boundary_contract (offline_boundary.rs:92-137) largely asserts the contract equals hardcoded constants (e.g. durable tables == ['kernel_crdt_updates','kernel_crdt_snapshots']). That part is tautological -- it proves the constructor matches itself, not that the system obeys the boundary. The MEANINGFUL proof is the runtime replay_offline_envelopes path, which is real. The contract object is a serialize-only descriptor for validators; treat it as documentation, not enforcement.

The denied-surface list (browser_local_storage, sqlite_file, filesystem_draft_files, markdown_mirror, provider_chat_history) correctly encodes the spec 2.3.13.11 prohibition; the cross-check that no denied surface appears in an allowed list (offline_boundary.rs:110-119) is a useful invariant guard.
evasion_probes: Tried to drive an authority mutation through offline replay: impossible -- the loop only calls push_yjs_update (draft log). No promotion/proposal writer reachable.
Tried to lose a buffered update silently: every envelope yields a verdict; denied tail is reported with reason, not dropped.

Tried to claim a denied durable surface (e.g. sqlite) as allowed in the contract: validate_offline_draft_boundary_contract rejects 'a denied surface appears in an allowed list' -- but this only guards the descriptor, not real storage.
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/crdt/yjs_bridge.rs:480-609 (push path the replay reuses) -- confirms draft-only

evidence: Adversarial code review PASS/PASS_WITH_NOTES (ADVERSARIAL_CODE_REVIEWER-20260611-PM); core sound, only defense-in-depth notes.

wp_validator_review_v1.review_scope: Validated only MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION in WP-KERNEL-009; no ACP used.
evidence_basis: Subagent and local WP Validator review of only the MTs that were READY_FOR_VALIDATION at session read.
Direct packet status/dependency map checked before mutation.

Targeted local cargo probes passed for MT-054, MT-056, and MT-063 hardening paths: relationship_id_resists_separator_injection_collision; db_blocks_retired_to_accepted_resurrection_via_raw_sql; scratch_schema_apply_rollback_reapply.
MT-049..MT-064 were independently confirmed by the returned subagent slice using real PostgreSQL-backed test paths.
wp_validator_review_v1.residual_uncertainty: Validation was scoped to MTs already READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Concurrent WIP/untracked files outside those MTs were not inspected.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
